gaps: No test case covers the 'Apps' settings section visibility in Device Settings for Voyager Focus 2 after pairing with Poly Studio Mobile app.
No test case verifies that all expected settings sections (including 'Apps') appear in the Device Settings screen for any Poly headset model.
No test case covers the Device Settings screen structure/completeness for Voyager Focus 2 specifically.
No test case validates that the 'Apps' section is present/absent based on device capabilities (e.g., Voyager Focus 2 supports Poly Lens integration which would require an 'Apps' section).

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Focus 2] Verify 'Apps' settings section visibility in Device Settings after initial pairing
steps: Ensure Poly Studio Mobile app is installed and not running (force-kill the app).
Ensure Voyager Focus 2 is powered off and not connected to any device.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS.
Tap 'Add Device' and follow the pairing flow to pair Voyager Focus 2 (put headset in pairing mode by pressing and holding the Bluetooth button for 5 seconds until LED flashes red/blue).
Complete the pairing and connection process.
On the Device screen, tap the connected Voyager Focus 2 device name.
Observe the Device Settings screen.
Verify that the 'Apps' section is visible in the list of settings sections.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' settings section is visible in the Device Settings screen for Voyager Focus 2 after initial pairing, indicating Poly Lens integration support is available.

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Focus 2] Verify 'Apps' section persists in Device Settings after app restart
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Focus 2 with Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS.
Navigate to Device screen → tap Voyager Focus 2 → confirm 'Apps' section is visible in Device Settings.
Press the Home button on iOS to background the app.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe up from app switcher).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for automatic reconnection to Voyager Focus 2 (or tap the device to reconnect).
Navigate to Device screen → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Verify that the 'Apps' section is still visible.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' settings section remains visible in Device Settings after app force-kill and relaunch.

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Focus 2] Verify 'Apps' section persists after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect cycle
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Focus 2 with Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS.
Navigate to Device screen → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings → confirm 'Apps' section is visible.
Turn off Voyager Focus 2 (slide power switch to OFF position).
Wait 10 seconds for the app to show 'Disconnected' status.
Turn on Voyager Focus 2 (slide power switch to ON position).
Wait for automatic Bluetooth reconnection (up to 30 seconds).
Navigate to Device screen → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Verify that the 'Apps' section is still visible.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' settings section remains visible in Device Settings after Bluetooth disconnect and reconnection.

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Focus 2] Verify 'Apps' section contains expected sub-settings or entry point
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Focus 2 with Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS.
Navigate to Device screen → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Locate the 'Apps' section in the settings list.
Tap on the 'Apps' section/row.
Observe the screen that opens.
generated_testcases.expected: Tapping the 'Apps' section opens a sub-screen showing available app integrations (e.g., Poly Lens, Calendar, or other supported apps) or provides a link to configure Poly Lens integration.

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Focus 2] Verify all expected Device Settings sections are present after pairing
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Focus 2 with Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS.
Navigate to Device screen → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Scroll through the entire Device Settings screen from top to bottom.
Verify the presence of each of the following sections: 'Audio Settings', 'Call Settings', 'Device Information', 'Apps', 'Device Actions' (or equivalent sections as per the app version).
Take note of any missing sections.
generated_testcases.expected: All expected Device Settings sections for Voyager Focus 2 are present, including 'Audio Settings', 'Call Settings', 'Device Information', 'Apps', and 'Device Actions'. The 'Apps' section is specifically present, confirming Poly Lens integration support.

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Focus 2] Verify 'Apps' section is absent for non-supported device (negative check)
steps: Pair and connect a device that does NOT support Poly Lens integration (e.g., Voyager 4320 or another non-Lens-supported model) with Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS.
Navigate to Device screen → tap the connected device → Device Settings.
Scroll through the entire Device Settings screen.
Verify whether an 'Apps' section is present or absent.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' section is NOT present in Device Settings for devices that do not support Poly Lens integration, confirming that the section is conditionally shown based on device capabilities.

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Focus 2] Verify 'Apps' section visibility after firmware update
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Focus 2 with Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS.
Navigate to Device screen → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings → confirm 'Apps' section is visible.
If a firmware update is available, navigate to Device Settings → Device Information → Software Update and install the latest firmware.
After firmware update completes and device reboots, wait for reconnection.
Navigate to Device screen → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Verify that the 'Apps' section is still visible after the firmware update.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' settings section remains visible in Device Settings after a firmware update is applied to Voyager Focus 2.

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Focus 2] Verify 'Apps' section visibility after unpairing and re-pairing
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Focus 2 with Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS.
Navigate to Device screen → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings → confirm 'Apps' section is visible.
Navigate to Device Settings → Device Actions → 'Forget Device' (or 'Remove Device').
Confirm the unpairing action.
Wait for the device to be removed from the app.
Put Voyager Focus 2 back into pairing mode (press and hold Bluetooth button for 5 seconds).
Tap 'Add Device' in the app and re-pair Voyager Focus 2.
Complete the pairing and connection process.
Navigate to Device screen → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Verify that the 'Apps' section is visible again.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' settings section is visible in Device Settings after unpairing and re-pairing Voyager Focus 2, confirming the section is correctly re-populated based on device capabilities.
